title: The Golden Age of Camelot Begins
body:
The great hall of Camelot blazes with torchlight and laughter. Arthur surveys his knights with quiet pride. The roads of Britain lie open before you — and the Well is out there somewhere, waiting.

Go forth. Adventure awaits.

title: The Quest of the Holy Grail Begins
body:
The Grail appeared at Pentecost and vanished before anyone could grasp it. In its wake, the land feels altered — as if something that was sleeping has woken, and something that was waking has gone to sleep.

The Well of Echoes, you have heard, is louder now than it was.

title: The Final Wars of Britain Begin
body:
The realm is fracturing. Armies move on roads that were made for pilgrims. And yet — the Well still stands. Some say the voice in it has changed. Some say it now speaks in names of the dead.

You ride toward it anyway.

title: The Well of Echoes
body:
The road bends through gorse and thorn and ends at a low stone wall ringing an old well. The water within is dark and perfectly still. A child's iron cup lies on the rim, dry.

As you approach, you hear — faintly, as if from far down the shaft — a voice calling a name you almost recognise.

responses.label: You may call down into the well and try to understand the voice.
You may draw water with the cup to see what the well gives up.
* You may lean over the rim and greet the voice as one greets a beloved.
You may leave the well and ride on.

title: A Voice from Below
body:
You lean over the stone and call. Your voice returns at once — and then again, late, changed. A question, not an echo: "Who asks?"

The hair lifts on your arms.

resolutions.label: answer with your true name and let faith guide you

success.body:
You give your name without hesitation. A long silence follows. Then the water far below brightens — not with sun, for the sky is grey — and a face looks up at you that is almost your own but older, and sadder, and kind.

"Then I will remember you," the face says, and is gone.

failure.body: You open your mouth and cannot find the words. After a long moment, the water stirs once and goes perfectly still. Whatever was listening has lost interest.

resolutions.label: puzzle out the meaning before speaking

success.body: You recognise the voice — not its owner, but its kind. A guardian of names. You answer with the old forms, and the water ripples once in acknowledgement. A cold silver ring rises to the surface and bobs there, waiting for you to take it.

failure.body: You take too long to think. The second echo comes again, sharper — "Who asks?" — and when you still do not answer, something beneath the water laughs without warmth. You step back from the rim, unsettled.

title: The Cup
body: The cup is lighter than iron should be. You lower it on its chain — longer than the well looks deep — and draw it up. The water within tastes of cold stone and, faintly, of salt, though you are a hundred leagues from any sea.

resolutions.label: read what you find in the cup

success.body: You see a single silver hair curled in the cup like a question mark. You understand at once — a debt that is not yours, waiting for someone to carry it. You return the hair to the dark with a short prayer, and ride away lighter than you came.

failure.body: You see nothing you can make sense of. You pocket a silver hair that was floating there — perhaps a keepsake, perhaps a trophy. That night, beside your fire, the hair is gone from your pouch and a fine new line has been drawn on the palm of your hand.

title: A Tender Greeting
body: You lean close and call down something warm and wordless — a greeting for a lover, not a stranger. A long pause. Then the well answers, and the answer is not a voice but a feeling, pressed gently against your chest like a hand.

resolutions.label: open your heart to whatever is below

success.body:
Laughter rises from the well — bright, surprised, not unkind.

"A bold one," the voice says. "Come back when you have earned a name worth calling."

The water stills. But you find you are smiling, and your heart is lighter than it was.

failure.body: You call out with all the warmth you can muster. The well sends your words back to you unchanged. Whatever is down there, it is not charmed by flattery. You straighten, cheeks burning, and mount your horse in silence.

title: The Road Onward
body:
You remount and ride. Within a mile the gorse gives way to tilled land and the sound of the well is gone from your ears, though not, perhaps, from your memory.

(End of encounter.)

title: The Well of Echoes — Age 2
body:
The well is exactly where you remember it, but the world around it has changed. The gorse is grey. The stone of the rim is darker, as though something has been seeping into it. The voice you heard before is still there — but now it speaks more urgently.

"You came back," it says. "Good. There is less time than there was."

responses.label: You may ask what it means by less time.
You may draw water again and see what it gives you now.
You may leave — you have enough to do without riddles.

title: The Well of Echoes — Age 3
body:
The road to the well is overgrown. You find it by memory, not by landmark. When you arrive, the stone wall has partially collapsed. The cup is gone.

But the voice is still there. It is quieter now, and it sounds tired.

responses.label: You may lean over the broken rim and call down.
You may sit beside the ruin and simply listen.
You may say your farewell and ride on.
